task.title: Make DecodeError a standard Rust error
task.objective: Let downstream typed event decoders compose DecodeError with ordinary Rust error surfaces without adapters.
acceptance_criteria: Both variants have deterministic Display text with useful context.
Tests prove the standard-error trait contract and exact messages.
The event-log replay contract remains unchanged.
The pinned local and required pull-request quality gates pass.

attempts.summary: Added the focused integration test before changing DecodeError.
attempts.diagnostic: Rust errors E0277 and E0599 reported that DecodeError implemented neither std::error::Error nor Display, so it could not be coerced to dyn Error or converted to a string.
attempts.patch: Added a trait assertion and exact-message checks for UnsupportedEvent and MalformedPayload.

attempts.summary: Implemented the minimum standard error compatibility and documented it.
attempts.patch: Added direct Display matching and an empty std::error::Error implementation in crates/vela-kernel/src/event_log.rs, plus the downstream compatibility note in docs/event-log.md.

attempts.summary: Reviewed the bounded diff and exercised debug, release, documentation, CI, and automated-review gates.
attempts.diagnostic: No blocking finding remained; CodeRabbit generated no actionable comments.
attempts.patch: Kept the implementation direct and added no conversions, variants, error sources, or replay changes.

outcome.summary: DecodeError now behaves as a conventional Rust error while preserving the existing decoder and replay boundaries.

verification.command: GitHub Rust quality gate for pull request 348
/run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # result: 6 valid, 0 invalid

lessons: Public decoder errors should implement Rust's standard error traits even when a higher layer maps them into richer replay errors.
A compile-time dyn Error coercion and exact variant messages provide a small deterministic test for both trait compatibility and diagnostic context.
Keep decoder failures narrow: standard error composition does not require adding storage, ordering, conversion, or source semantics.
